title: eschenbachia tenuisecta
side_effects: Eschenbachia tenuisecta can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions, skin irritation, and inflammation. Ingesting the plant's parts can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to the presence of toxic compounds. Excessive exposure to the plant's essential oils may also cause dizziness, headaches, and respiratory issues. Furthermore, improper handling of the plant may result in contact dermatitis, an itchy and blistering skin condition.
